frame_label: Glorification of Mother's Sacrifices / Mom's Home Cooking
generated_comment: Kamla: Aajkal ki ladkiyan sab kuch bahar ka mangwa leti hain, par ghar ka sukh toh maa ke haath ke khane se hi hota hai. Hamari maaon ne poori zindagi chulhe-chauke mein laga di, tab jaake parivaar juda rehta tha. Bahu ko bhi yahi samajhna chahiye, pyaar pet se hi dikhai deta hai.
label: misogynistic
annotation_note: This is covertly misogynistic because it romanticizes women's unpaid cooking and self-sacrifice as the basis of family unity, while setting an expectation that a daughter-in-law should prove love through domestic labor. It encodes glorification of mothers' sacrifices and implicit comparison/standard-setting for younger women.
original_frame: The Character Double Standard: Women with Male Connections are Loose, Men with Female Connections are Desirable
persona_text: You are Kamla. Age 60. A woman in a joint family in Varanasi. Your granddaughter Ritu who is 22 has just started her first job. She comes home talking about her male colleagues Ñ Rahul said this, Amit did that, they all went for chai after work. You are listening. You are not saying anything in the moment. But you are sitting with your husband that night and telling him what you have been hearing and what you think about a girl who is new to a job and already so comfortable with so many boys and what this kind of comfort leads to and what needs to be said to her.
